article_title: WordPress 7.0 Drops Real-Time Collaboration Twelve Days Before Its May 20 Release as Mullenweg Pulls the Cornerstone Feature
summary: Duplicate of the already-published News article on the same WordPress 7.0 RTC cut (PR #1268). Same event, same framing, four of six sources overlap; the published sibling is the stronger piece. Rejected as redundant coverage.

content_preview.title: WordPress 7.0 Drops Real-Time Collaboration Twelve Days Before Its May 20 Release as Mullenweg Pulls the Cornerstone Feature
content_preview.summary: WordPress 7.0 ships May 20, 2026, but its long-planned real-time co-editing feature was cut from core on May 8 over robustness concerns.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

WordPress 7.0 is scheduled for final release on May 20, 2026, according to [WordPress.org News](https://wordpress.org/news/2026/05/wordpress-7-0-release-candidate-3/). But the version will ship without real-time collaboration, the simultaneous multi-user editing feature that contributors had spent years building. On May 8, 2026, the WordPress core team confirmed that "Real Time Collaboration will not be included in the 7.0 release and will be re-evaluated during the 7.1 release cycl...

recommendations: No rewrite — the WordPress 7.0 RTC cut is already covered by the published News article 2026-05/14-wordpress-70-ships-may-20-without-real-time-collaboration-after-matt-mullenweg-pulls-the-phase-3-flagship-12-days-before-release, and the broader release by the published Analysis piece 2026-05/14-wordpress-70-ships-may-20-with-a-provider-agnostic-ai-client-in-core-an-admin-refresh-and-no-real-time-collaboration.

editor_notes.content_quality: The article is competently written and well-structured (757 words, News). Quality is not the reason for rejection.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 6 source snapshots present and fetched at HTTP 200. Spot-checked the snapshots: source-0 (WordPress.org RC3) confirms '143+ issues addressed since RC2', the 'no longer considered a new Beta 1' line, and the verbatim 'Real Time Collaboration will not be included in the 7.0 release and will be re-evaluated during the 7.1 release cycle'; source-4 (Developer Blog Abilities API intro) confirms the 'first-class, cross-context functional API' quote, the 'central registry where WordPress capabilities...' quote, the 'WordPress doesn't currently force a standard method...' quote, and that the server-side PHP implementation 'was recently approved to be merged in WordPress 6.9' — all verbatim. Source overlap with the published News sibling (#1268) is heavy: WordPress.org RC3, the Make Core schedule, the Make Core RTC-removal post, and Search Engine Journal are all shared; the client-side Abilities API note and the Abilities API intro are shared with the published Analysis sibling (#1266).
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: No fabrication detected. The facts are accurate; they are simply already published.
editor_notes.originality: FAILS. This submission covers the exact same event as the article published from PR #1268 — the May 8, 2026 removal of real-time collaboration from WordPress 7.0, framed on the May 20 release date. Same lead, same Mullenweg-pulls-the-feature framing, same core facts (the robustness-concerns list, the unchanged ship date, the Abilities API as 'what still ships'), and four of six sources overlap. It is the weaker of the two RTC-cut News pieces: it does not cite the dedicated RTC performance-testing analysis (#1268 does, and uses it for substantive detail), and it carries materially less on the rest of the 7.0 feature set. Under the v3.9.0 originality rule this is a duplicate.
editor_notes.quote_audit: Rule 3 check passed for the quotes spot-checked — every quoted passage reproduces source text verbatim with correct attribution. No paraphrase-inside-quote-marks, no misattribution. Rejection is purely on originality grounds, not quote integrity.
concerns: Duplicate of an already-published News article covering the identical WordPress 7.0 RTC-removal event, with heavily overlapping sources.
recommendations: No rewrite. The WordPress 7.0 story is already covered from two angles — a News piece on the RTC cut (#1268) and an Analysis piece on the AI Client architecture (#1266). #1268 was selected over this submission as the stronger RTC-cut News piece because it cites the dedicated performance-testing analysis and the Gutenberg Times tracker, and carries more detail on what still ships in 7.0.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: REJECT as a duplicate. The work is competent but redundant — the same RTC-cut story is already live in #1268. Under v3.9.0 there is no rewrite cycle for duplicates.
